# Fenrir Web Framework

Fenrir is a high-performance, hybrid Python web framework built on top of modern ASGI specifications. It elegantly merges the context-locals of Flask, automatic Pydantic validation of FastAPI, high-speed class views of Falcon, and async listeners/websockets of Sanic into a single unified event loop.

## Core Paradigms

- **Flask-Style**: Context-local requests (`from fenrir import request, g`).
- **FastAPI-Style**: Pydantic type hinting for automatic request body validation.
- **Falcon-Style**: Low-overhead Class-Based Resource Controllers (`on_get`, `on_post`).
- **Sanic/ASGI-Style**: High-performance async event stream (SSE) and low-level WebSockets.

## Quick Start

```python
from fenrir import Fenrir, request
from pydantic import BaseModel

app = Fenrir(title="Fenrir App")

class User(BaseModel):
    username: str

@app.post("/user")
async def create_user(body: User):
    return {"status": "created", "data": body.model_dump()}

@app.get("/")
async def index():
    return {"framework": "Fenrir", "speed": "Ultra High"}
